headline: Battery deal surge dominates Australian renewable energy market
summary: Investor activity in Australian energy storage is intensifying with multiple transactions — purchases, sales, financing arrangements, and PPAs for big batteries. BESS dominated investment in Q4 2025 with 16 deals totalling 2.16 GW across the NEM. Analysts expect the pace to accelerate in 2026 as CIS-awarded projects approach FID deadlines.

headline: Victoria's second-largest battery connects to grid, household storage demand hits record
summary: A major BESS facility adjacent to Victoria's largest gas generation infrastructure connected to the grid management system, accelerating BESS deployment across NEM. Household battery rebate demand in Victoria simultaneously hit record levels, reflecting the sharp decline in residential battery costs. The dual dynamic is reshaping the Victorian grid's flexibility profile.

headline: NSW Planning Minister clears three wind projects after legal challenges resolved
summary: NSW Planning Minister resolved legal challenges blocking three wind energy development applications, advancing projects toward approval. The decision signals continued government support for wind development in regional NSW despite community opposition in some areas. Approvals are expected to unlock over 1 GW of new wind capacity in the state.

headline: Fortescue begins construction of Western Australia's largest solar facility
summary: Fortescue commenced construction of Western Australia's largest solar facility as part of its Pilbara decarbonisation 'real zero' initiative. The project will supply renewable power to Fortescue's mining operations and forms a key plank of the company's strategy to eliminate fossil fuels from its operations by 2030. The facility will include co-located battery storage.

headline: Spanish RE company completes acquisition of Australian wind farm, plans grid upgrades
summary: A Spanish renewable energy company completed the purchase of a formerly record-holding Australian wind farm, with plans to alleviate grid constraints through transmission upgrades. The deal was cleared by ACCC under the new mandatory merger control regime that took effect January 2026. The buyer plans to expand the wind farm's capacity and add co-located battery storage.

headline: Sembcorp shareholders approve A$6.5B Alinta Energy acquisition at EGM
summary: Sembcorp Industries received EGM shareholder approval for the landmark A$6.5B Alinta Energy acquisition. Completion remains subject to FIRB and ACCC approvals, with both regulatory processes ongoing as at March 2026. Alinta's 10.4 GW renewable pipeline and 3,400 MW of existing generation capacity make this the largest Asian strategic entry into the Australian power market.

headline: ACCC mandatory merger control regime takes effect from 1 January 2026
summary: Australia's new mandatory merger clearance regime came into force on 1 January 2026, requiring ACCC approval before completing transactions crossing certain thresholds. The regime has particular implications for battery storage, grid infrastructure, and cross-border energy transactions. Advisers warn the new regime will add 3-6 months to deal timelines and increase regulatory risk for large RE transactions.

headline: CIS Tender 8 opens — government seeks 16 GWh in largest single storage procurement globally
summary: The federal government opened CIS Tender 8, targeting 16 GWh of new NEM battery storage — the largest single CIS storage procurement to date and one of the largest government storage tenders globally. Results are expected mid-2026. The tender signals the government's commitment to accelerating BESS deployment as coal retirements escalate and grid reliability concerns grow.

headline: Wholesale electricity prices near halved as record renewables surge across NEM
summary: NEM wholesale electricity averaged AU$50/MWh in the December 2025 quarter — nearly half the prior year average — driven by record renewable output. Renewables and storage delivered 51% of NEM supply in the quarter, a historic milestone. The price decline is squeezing merchant revenue assumptions and accelerating demand for longer-duration BESS with firming capability.

headline: Sembcorp announces A$6.5B Alinta Energy acquisition — largest Asian strategic deal in Australian power
summary: Singapore's Sembcorp Industries signed a share sale agreement to acquire 100% of Alinta Energy from Chow Tai Fook Enterprises for A$6.5B enterprise value. The deal supports Sembcorp's target of 25 GW renewables capacity by 2028 and is immediately EPS accretive. Funded fully in cash via bridge facility — FIRB and ACCC approvals pending.

headline: Record Q4 2025: 1.8 GW commissioned in NEM, BESS claims 7 of the projects
summary: Q4 2025 was the strongest quarter on record for NEM capacity additions, with 1.8 GW commissioned including 7 BESS projects. Full FY2024-25 saw a record 4.4 GW commissioned. The grid connection pipeline hit a record 64 GW — up 14% quarter-on-quarter — with battery storage claiming a 46% share of pipeline capacity.

headline: CIS Tender 7 closes — government targets 5 GW new generation, results due May 2026
summary: Bidding closed for CIS Tender 7, which targets 5 GW of new NEM generation with results expected May 2026. The tender received strong interest from wind, solar, and hybrid developers. Combined CIS awards now total 13 GW generation plus 5.78 GW / 21.57 GWh of dispatchable capacity since the scheme launched.

headline: Origin Energy commissions 460 MW / 1.84 GWh Eraring BESS — coal-to-battery model proven
summary: Origin Energy commissioned the 460 MW / 1.84 GWh Eraring BESS at the site of the retiring Eraring coal power station, establishing a replicable coal-to-BESS repurposing model. The project repurposes existing grid connection infrastructure and community assets at the retiring coal site. Similar approaches are being assessed at Yallourn and Callide B as those plants approach closure.

headline: Project EnergyConnect commissioning begins — first power flows on SA-NSW interconnector
summary: Project EnergyConnect, the new A$2.3B interconnector linking South Australia and NSW via ElectraNet and TransGrid, commenced commissioning with first power flows achieved in November 2025. The interconnector will significantly increase renewable energy transfer between SA — which already exceeds 70% renewables — and NSW. Full commercial operation is expected in 2026.

headline: Australia surpasses UK to become world's 3rd-largest utility BESS market
summary: Australia overtook the UK to become the world's third-largest market for utility-scale battery energy storage systems, trailing only China and the United States. The milestone was driven by record 2025 commissioning and a deep pipeline of CIS-contracted projects. Australia is also the first nation to exceed 1 GWh of utility battery capacity per million people.

headline: CIS Tender 3 awards 4.13 GW / 15.37 GWh BESS — largest battery tender in history
summary: The federal government awarded CIS Tender 3 contracts to 16 battery projects across the NEM — 5 in NSW, 5 in VIC, 4 in QLD, and 2 in SA — totalling 4.13 GW / 15.37 GWh. This is the largest single battery storage tender ever awarded globally. Key winners include projects backed by Akaysha Energy (BlackRock/GIP), Octopus Energy, and Quinbrook.

headline: Western Downs BESS Stage 2 commissioned six weeks ahead of schedule
summary: Neoen (now Brookfield) commissioned the 270 MW / 540 MWh Western Downs Battery Stage 2 in Queensland six weeks ahead of schedule. The project demonstrates improving construction efficiencies in Australian BESS delivery as supply chains normalise. Brookfield's Australian BESS portfolio now exceeds 700 MW of operational capacity.

headline: HumeLink 500 kV construction advancing — key link for Snowy 2.0 power transfer
summary: Transgrid's HumeLink 500 kV transmission line — a critical piece of infrastructure connecting Snowy 2.0 to Sydney and Canberra load centres — is advancing through the construction phase with in-service date targeting 2028-2029. The A$3.3B+ project has faced delays and cost increases but remains essential for unlocking pumped hydro capacity. Land access negotiations with landowners continue.

headline: Waratah Super Battery Stage 1 energised — world's largest single-site BESS registered in NEM
summary: Akaysha Energy (BlackRock/GIP) energised the first 350 MW / 700 MWh stage of the Waratah Super Battery in the Hunter Valley, NSW, and registered it in the NEM. The full 850 MW / 1.6 GWh project — featuring 3,598 battery units and 288 Hitachi inverters — is expected online in 2026. The project holds a CIS Tender 3 contract and connects to the TransGrid network.

headline: CPP Investments acquires Neoen Victoria portfolio for A$950M
summary: CPP Investments (Canada Pension Plan) acquired the Neoen Victoria portfolio for A$950M, comprising 652 MW including the Victorian Big Battery (300 MW/450 MWh), Bulgana Wind Farm, and Numurkah Solar Farm. The deal validates premium pricing for operating BESS assets in the NEM. This was a carve-out from the broader Brookfield/Neoen transaction.

headline: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners raises A$400M for Australian wind and BESS pipeline
summary: Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners closed a A$400M capital raise to fund continued Australian wind and BESS investment. The specialist RE fund manager now has 1.5 GW+ of operational and construction-stage assets and a 5 GW development pipeline including the Supernode BESS and multiple wind projects. Quinbrook holds CIS Tender 3 BESS contracts.

headline: APG commits USD 650M to Octopus Energy Australia — Hanworth BESS acquired
summary: Dutch pension giant APG invested USD 650M in Octopus Energy's Australian platform, making it one of the largest single capital commitments to Australian BESS. Octopus subsequently acquired the 1.2 GW / 4.8 GWh Hanworth BESS project in NSW — Australia's largest planned battery storage project. The project holds a CIS Tender 3 contract.

headline: CIS target uplifted to 40 GW — federal government raises ambition by 25%
summary: The federal government uplifted the Capacity Investment Scheme target from 32 GW to 40 GW of new capacity by 2030 — a 25% increase. The uplift was accompanied by CIS Tender 4 awards of 6.6 GW renewable generation across 20 projects, 12 of which are hybrid solar and battery. Total expected investment under CIS is now A$73B+.

headline: CDPQ acquires Edify Energy in landmark A$1.1B+ deal — Canadian pension deploys in AU BESS
summary: La Caisse de depot et placement du Quebec (CDPQ) agreed to acquire Edify Energy, a major Australian solar and battery storage developer with 11 GW+ pipeline and multiple CIS contracts. The deal values Edify at approximately A$1.1B+, reflecting a premium for CIS-contracted development pipeline. CDPQ rebranded to 'La Caisse' reflecting its global infrastructure ambitions.

headline: Marinus Link FID delayed — Tasmanian undersea cable under government review
summary: The A$3.8B Marinus Link undersea cable project linking Tasmania to Victoria has had its final investment decision delayed as governments review project economics in light of changing market conditions and cost escalation. The project would unlock Tasmania's hydropower as a 'battery of the nation' but faces questions about merchant viability given lower wholesale prices. A revised business case is expected later in 2025.

headline: Brookfield completes EUR 6.1B Neoen acquisition — Neoen delists from Euronext Paris
summary: Brookfield Renewable Holdings (with Temasek) completed the full EUR 6.1B (~A$10B) acquisition of Neoen via mandatory squeeze-out, with Neoen delisting from Euronext Paris on April 4, 2025. The landmark transaction is among the largest renewable energy M&A deals globally. Neoen's Australian assets include Hornsdale Power Reserve and the Victorian Big Battery.

headline: FIRB scrutiny intensifies on Chinese-linked RE investments — multiple bids rejected or deterred
summary: FIRB rejected or deterred multiple bids from Chinese state-owned or linked entities for Australian renewable energy assets on national security grounds. The A$0 threshold for foreign government investors continues to apply to electricity generation. Advisers note that Chinese SOE-backed consortia are effectively excluded from Australian RE M&A, reshaping the competitive landscape for asset sales.

headline: Torrens Island BESS operational — AGL's 250 MW / 1 GWh flagship storage asset online
summary: AGL Energy commissioned the Torrens Island BESS (250 MW / 1,000 MWh) in South Australia, replacing retiring gas generation capacity at the same site. The project uses Fluence technology and holds a CIS contract. With SA already exceeding 70% renewables penetration, the Torrens Island BESS plays a key stabilising role for the state's grid.

headline: Star of the South offshore wind — Victorian offshore wind zone confirmed, leasing underway
summary: The federal government confirmed the Gippsland Offshore Wind Zone in Victoria and opened the offshore wind leasing process. Star of the South — Australia's most advanced offshore wind project at 2.2 GW — is positioned as a frontrunner for early lease allocation. The zone could host up to 13 GW of offshore wind, supporting Victoria's 95% renewables target by 2035.

headline: AEMO ISP 2025 projects NEM capacity to triple — 297 GW by 2050, 40 GW BESS required
summary: AEMO's 2025 Integrated System Plan projects total NEM capacity will need to triple from approximately 92 GW today to 297 GW by 2050 to meet decarbonisation targets. Grid-scale wind and solar must reach 120 GW by 2050, while 40 GW of battery storage will be required — a 433% increase from the current ~7.5 GW fleet. Annual investment of A$10B+ per year in generation and storage is needed.

headline: CIS Tenders 5 & 6 close in WEM — 1.6 GW generation and 2,400 MWh storage sought
summary: CIS Tenders 5 and 6, targeting the Western Australian Wholesale Electricity Market (WEM), closed in November 2025 with results expected March-April 2026. The tenders collectively seek 1.6 GW of new generation and 2,400 MWh of storage. The WEM has lagged the NEM in BESS deployment and the tenders represent a significant market opening for storage developers in WA.
